body_txt: In most cases, cheating on a partner is seen as a pretty scummy thing to do. Usually, the cheater will at least be aware that there'll be consequences; even if they couldn't care less about their partner's feelings, they at least don't want to get caught. The point is that a normal way to handle it is to hide the infidelity by any means necessary, even if they're otherwise very callous about it. This means that it's shocking if someone is nasty enough to cheat on their partner openly . In most cases, they do the act in a spot where their partner could catch them, occasionally with the intention to be seen. Other times, they'll do the bare minimum and have their affair in a private setting, but be completely honest if confronted; if not confronted, this becomes something of an open secret. The key is that the cheating partner is fully aware that they either can or will be exposed, and they're okay with it. No lies, no secrecy, no attempts to justify or gaslight ; they're just honest, but in a way that makes their actions even nastier. If they do lie, it'll be in an effort to win their partner back, such as by promising they'll never do it again — but if they've already been that petty enough to cheat in the open once, such promises are doomed to fail the second they feel like they can get away with it again. That's the real core of the trope: They cheat because they can, and do it shamelessly because they're just that okay with hurting their partner, sometimes even over and over again . Far from being concerned about getting caught, they're willing to rub their unfaithfulness in their partner's face and show no true remorse for it if confronted. While the reasons for it can vary, it boils down to two key elements: they don't care about their partner's feelings, and they aren't fazed by the threat of being caught. Sometimes, they're aware that the consequences aren't real for one reason or another, so they'll do whatever they want with no shame. Other times, they're just a nasty piece of work, such as a Sadist or The Sociopath , and they either want to cause harm or don't feel bad for doing so. The reaction of the victim can also vary. In many cases, they're unwilling to stand up for themselves , or may just be entirely unable to act; whether it's due to fear, love, coercion, or even the law, they end up trapped in this situation with no real way to escape it. This can lead to them suffering passively, maybe even as an Emasculated Cuckold . Of course, there are times when the victim will take a stand, and end the relationship or otherwise fight back — sometimes, literally. Regardless, they're the sympathetic party of the story, even if other characters take to Blaming the Cuckold ... Unless, of course, both partners are mutually toxic in this regard. This sort of thing can go hand-in-hand with Domestic Abuse , as the abuser will care so little for their partner's feelings that cheating on them is simply another form of torment, a way to emphasize their worthlessness in the relationship. Even if the partner isn't an abuser, however, doing this still showcases how little they actually love the person they're with; they can't even put in the effort to pretend to care for them, let alone respect them. Downplayed examples will "merely" involve open flirting, which is still grossly disrespectful, and is the first step to any "real" cheating anyway. Note that this doesn't apply to characters who are in a consensual polyamorous or open relationship. In that case, the characters in question are simply abiding by the boundaries they agreed upon and seeing other people because they're allowed to (Which can sometimes fall under My Girl Is a Slut instead). It can only apply in cases where there's a previous agreement that what they're doing is off-limits; lines need to be crossed for the characterization to work. Mistaking polyamory and/or an open relationship for this trope falls under Mistaken for Cheating . Compare Intentional Heartbreaker and Serial Homewrecker . This has a tendency to pop up in Netorare . Do not confuse with someone who cheats in a competition in a callous and casual way, though that too is a sign of a Jerkass .
